name: Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B
license: Tongyi Qianwen LICENSE AGREEMENT (custom, free commercial use below usage threshold)
archDisplay: Fine-grained MoE, decoder-only, upcycled from Qwen-1.8B: 64 experts (4 always-on shared + 60 routed, 4 routed activated per token)
note: First Qwen MoE model: 14.3B total/2.7B active params match dense Qwen1.5-7B quality at ~25% of its training cost and 1.74x inference throughput.
tpsNote: VRAM sized on the 14.3B total weights (all experts must stay resident even though only 2.7B are active per token); at release only HF transformers and vLLM were supported, llama.cpp/GGUF support came later.

results.notes: FP16 baseline row, Table 10 (Appendix A.7) of KBVQ-MoE (ICLR 2026, Xu/Zhao/Hu/Chen/Yang, Houmo AI + NTU) -- a third-party MoE-quantization paper using the LM-Evaluation-Harness toolkit on the base model. Verified via direct PDF read of arXiv:2602.11184. Independent of Alibaba's own launch-blog figures already cited for mmlu/gsm8k/humaneval.
FP16 baseline row, Table 10 (Appendix A.7) of KBVQ-MoE (ICLR 2026). Same eval run as the other arc-easy/hellaswag/lambada/piqa/winogrande entries added alongside this one.

Labeled 'HE' in the paper's Table 10 (confirmed via section 5 text listing 'Arc-Challenge, Arc-Easy, HellaSwag, LAMBADA-openai, LAMBADA-standard, PIQA, WinoGrande' as the 7 zero-shot tasks -- not to be confused with HumanEval, which this table does not include). FP16 baseline row.

Reported as 'LAMBADA-openai' in Table 10 (the more commonly cited LAMBADA variant). A second 'LAMBADA-standard' split scored 64.62 in the same table but is not separately mapped since the taxonomy has a single 'lambada' slug.
FP16 baseline row, Table 10 (Appendix A.7) of KBVQ-MoE (ICLR 2026).
Labeled 'WI' in the paper's Table 10. FP16 baseline row of KBVQ-MoE (ICLR 2026).

MT-Bench score for the Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B-Chat (instruction-tuned) variant, from the same official launch blog already used as this model's primary spec source (specSources.releaseDate). The page's benchmark table was previously only mined for the 3 base-model results (mmlu/gsm8k/humaneval); this chat-model MT-Bench figure sits on the same page but had not been captured.

verificationNotes: Fetched qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen-moe/ directly: confirms release 2024-03-28, 14.3B total/2.7B activated params, 64 fine-grained experts (4 shared always-on + 60 routed, 4 activated/token), upcycled from Qwen-1.8B, ~25% training cost and 1.74x throughput vs Qwen1.5-7B. All 3 cited result rows (MMLU 62.5, GSM8K 61.5, HumanEval 34.2) appear verbatim on the page. 32K context corroborated by qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen1.5/ (all Qwen1.5 models support 32768 tokens). No changes made.
